changelog:
Streaming reliability + bulk import + auto-disable on full quota.

Streaming truncation fix:
- Replaced suffix-overlap heuristic in normalizeChunk with a delta-vs-cumulative-replay reconciler. The old heuristic dropped letters when an upstream replay coincidentally shared a prefix with the new delta ("sleep" → "slep", "lets begin" → "letsbegin"). The new reconciler tracks emitted bytes per text block and correctly distinguishes deltas from cumulative replays.
- Deduped the inline thinking-tag streamer between the Anthropic and OpenAI paths into a single thinkingTextProcessor state machine. Both streams now share one implementation, eliminating drift.

Bulk import (export/import round-trip):
- New POST /api/accounts/import endpoint accepts the existing export envelope ({version, exportedAt, accounts}). Default merge strategy is skip-on-duplicate-refreshToken — operators can no longer accidentally overwrite local credentials by re-importing an old export. Selectable: skip / overwrite / dry-run.
- Always re-derives accessToken via auth.RefreshToken on import; never trusts the file's stored accessToken (could be stale or belong to a different machine fingerprint).
- New dashboard import modal mirrors the export flow with file picker, per-account selection, dedupe preview, and dry-run. XSS-safe (textContent for all account-supplied fields).
- Handles UTF-8 BOM in uploaded files.
- Detects and skips rotated-RT duplicates (same email + different RT but same accessToken signature).

Auto-disable on full quota:
- Accounts at full usage (UsageCurrent >= UsageLimit, or trial equivalents) now auto-disable on the next refresh and skip the routing pool entirely. The state machine respects operator intent: a manually-disabled account is never auto-recovered, and an auto-disabled account auto-recovers when quota drops below the limit (e.g. monthly billing reset).
- Limits flow per-account from the upstream getUsageLimits API — no magic constants. Free 50, Pro 1000, Pro+ 2000+, custom enterprise tiers, and fractional credit limits are all handled by the same `UsageCurrent >= UsageLimit` predicate.
- Interaction with overage: per-account AllowOverage and global AllowOverUsage suppress auto-disable at full quota, so accounts flagged for overage stay in rotation at their reduced overage weight (1..10) as the operator intended. Turning overage on after an auto-disable triggers immediate recovery.
- Trial-only accounts (UsageLimit=0, TrialUsageLimit>0) are covered by the same machine.
- New tests: 9 unit tests on applyAutoDisableTransition (every state-table cell), 4 integration tests on UpdateAccountInfo, and a no-magic-constant pin that exercises the predicate at limits 5, 500, 2000, 5000, 100000, and fractional 12.5.
